name: oracle-sqlcl
description: Oracle database access via SQLcl built-in MCP server
long_description:
Enables Claude to connect to Oracle databases using Oracle SQLcl's built-in MCP server. Supports named connections, SQL queries, PL/SQL execution and SQLcl commands.

**REQUIREMENTS**
- Oracle SQLcl 25.2+ installed
- Java 17+ JRE installed
- Named connections configured in SQLcl

**RESTRICT LEVELS**
- 4 (default): Most restrictive — blocks CONNMGR, scripts, host commands
- 3: Blocks host commands and scripts
- 2: Blocks only host commands
- 1: Allows scripts, blocks host commands
- 0: Full access — all SQLcl commands including CONNMGR

sqlcl_path.title: SQLcl executable path
sqlcl_path.description: Full path to the SQLcl executable (sql or sql.exe). Use 'sql' if SQLcl is in PATH.

restrict_level.title: Restrict Level (0-4)
restrict_level.description: Controls which SQLcl commands are available via MCP. Enter a value from 0 to 4: 0 = full access (CONNMGR rename/add/delete, scripts, host), 1 = allows scripts, 2 = blocks host cmds, 3 = blocks scripts+host, 4 = most restrictive (default, no CONNMGR write ops).

prompts.name: add-connection
prompts.description: Add a new Oracle database connection

prompts.name: rename-connection
prompts.description: Rename an existing Oracle database connection

prompts.name: delete-connection
prompts.description: Delete an existing Oracle database connection
prompts.text:
Delete the Oracle SQLcl connection named '${arguments.name}'.

First list all connections so the user can confirm this is the right one.
Then use the run-sqlcl tool and execute: connmgr delete -conn ${arguments.name}

After executing, verify by listing all connections.

prompts.name: list-connections
prompts.description: List all saved Oracle database connections with details
prompts.text: List all saved Oracle SQLcl connections with their full details (host, port, service, username) using the list-connections tool with show_details=true.

tools.name: list-connections
tools.description: List all saved SQLcl named connections

tools.name: connect
tools.description: Connect to a named Oracle database connection

tools.name: run-sql
tools.description: Execute SQL or PL/SQL on the connected Oracle database

tools.name: run-sqlcl
tools.description: Execute SQLcl-specific commands (INFO, DESC, CTAS, CONNMGR...)

tools.name: disconnect
tools.description: Disconnect from the current Oracle database session
